description_long: This object is a vintage-style corded telephone with a robust, angular black base and a classic rotary dial at the center. The base features an array of metallic buttons and indicator lights, some of which are green and others white or gray, suggesting a multi-line or switchboard function. The handset is black, resting on a cradle above the rotary dial, and is supported by a sturdy metal bracket. The surface of the base appears to be made of a dark, slightly glossy material, possibly Bakelite or painted metal, with a subtle texture. The overall design is utilitarian and solid, evoking early to mid-20th-century telecommunications equipment. This type of phone would likely be found in an office, control room, or other professional setting.
description: A vintage black corded telephone with a rotary dial, multiple metallic buttons, indicator lights, and a classic handset resting on a cradle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, handset on cradle, and a full array of buttons and indicator lights.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the angled profile of the base and the side of the handset.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the back of the handset and the solid, sloped back of the base.
description_view_3: Right side view displaying the side profile of the handset and the button array from a different angle.
